contentData.apache_config:
Mutex file:${APACHE_LOCK_DIR} default
PidFile ${APACHE_PID_FILE}

Timeout 300
KeepAlive On
MaxKeepAliveRequests 500
KeepAliveTimeout 15

User ${APACHE_RUN_USER}
Group ${APACHE_RUN_GROUP}

##### E2 CUSTOM SETTINGS #####
UseCanonicalName On
ServerTokens Prod
ServerSignature EMail
TraceEnable Off

# Fargate/Docker environment variables
PassEnv AWS_CONTAINER_CREDENTIALS_RELATIVE_URI
PassEnv AWS_DEFAULT_REGION
PassEnv E2_DOCKER
PassEnv E2_MAINTENANCE_MESSAGE
PassEnv E2_DBSERV

HostnameLookups Off
AccessFileName .htaccess
<Files ~ "^\.ht">
	Order allow,deny
	Deny from all
	Satisfy all
</Files>

# PSGI/Starman: Apache is a pure reverse proxy to the Starman backend on :5000
# (started by apache2_wrapper.rb); it runs NO app code and mod_perl is not loaded.
# The legacy mod_perl path and its E2_PSGI toggle were removed in the full PSGI
# commit (#4234) -- this config is now PSGI-only, on mpm_event.


Define E2_DEVELOPMENT
UnDefine E2_PRODUCTION


# Include module configuration
Include mods-enabled/*.load
Include mods-enabled/*.conf

# mod_remoteip - Use X-Forwarded-For header as client IP
# Required for mod_evasive to rate-limit by real client IP (not ALB IP)
<IfModule mod_remoteip.c>
	RemoteIPHeader X-Forwarded-For
	# Trust private/internal IPs (ALB, Docker, localhost)
	RemoteIPTrustedProxy 10.0.0.0/8
	RemoteIPTrustedProxy 172.16.0.0/12
	RemoteIPTrustedProxy 192.168.0.0/16
	RemoteIPTrustedProxy 127.0.0.1

	# CloudFront IP ranges (fetched from AWS at config generation time)

	RemoteIPTrustedProxy 23.228.249.0/24

	RemoteIPTrustedProxy 120.52.22.96/27

	RemoteIPTrustedProxy 23.228.222.0/24

	RemoteIPTrustedProxy 205.251.249.0/24

</IfModule>

# mod_evasive rate limiting configuration
# Protects against DoS attacks and aggressive scrapers
<IfModule mod_evasive20.c>

	# Development: Very high thresholds to avoid test interference
	DOSHashTableSize 3097
	DOSPageCount 99999
	DOSPageInterval 1
	DOSSiteCount 99999
	DOSSiteInterval 1
	DOSBlockingPeriod 1

</IfModule>

LogLevel warn
LogFormat "{\"time\":\"%{%Y-%m-%d}tT%{%T}t.%{msec_frac}tZ\",\"process\":\"%D\",\"filename\":\"%f\",\"remoteIP\":\"%{X-Forwarded-For}i\",\"host\":\"%V\",\"request\":\"%U\",\"query\":\"%q\",\"method\":\"%m\",\"status\":\"%>s\",\"userAgent\":\"%{User-agent}i\",\"referer\":\"%{Referer}i\"}" cloudwatch

BrowserMatch "^ELB-HealthChecker" loghealthcheck

<IfDefine E2_DEVELOPMENT>
ErrorLogFormat "{\"time\":\"%{%usec_frac}t\", \"function\" : \"[%-m:%l]\", \"process\" : \"[pid%P]\" ,\"message\" : \"%M\"}"
ErrorLog "|/usr/bin/rotatelogs -f /var/log/apache2/e2.error.%Y%m%d%H.log 3600"
SetEnvIf Remote_Addr "127\.0\.0\.1" dontlog
SetEnvIf Request_URI "^/server_live\.html$" dontlog
SetEnvIf Request_URI "favicon.ico$" dontlog
SetEnvIf Request_URI "^/robots\.txt$" dontlog
</IfDefine>

<IfDefine E2_PRODUCTION>
Define E2StartServers 2
Define E2ServerLimit 4
Define E2ThreadsPerChild 25
Define E2MinSpareThreads 25
Define E2MaxSpareThreads 75
Define E2MaxRequestWorkers 100
Define E2MaxConnections 5000
</IfDefine>

<IfDefine E2_DEVELOPMENT>
Define E2StartServers 1
Define E2ServerLimit 2
Define E2ThreadsPerChild 25
Define E2MinSpareThreads 10
Define E2MaxSpareThreads 50
Define E2MaxRequestWorkers 50
Define E2MaxConnections 0
</IfDefine>

# mpm_event: Apache is a pure proxy under PSGI (no mod_perl, which is what
# mandated prefork). Threads handle client connections cheaply; effective request
# concurrency is bounded by STARMAN_WORKERS at the backend, so MaxRequestWorkers
# is generous headroom for client connections, not parallel app work.
<IfModule mpm_event_module>
StartServers            ${E2StartServers}
ServerLimit             ${E2ServerLimit}
ThreadLimit             ${E2ThreadsPerChild}
ThreadsPerChild         ${E2ThreadsPerChild}
MinSpareThreads         ${E2MinSpareThreads}
MaxSpareThreads         ${E2MaxSpareThreads}
MaxRequestWorkers       ${E2MaxRequestWorkers}
MaxConnectionsPerChild  ${E2MaxConnections}
</IfModule>

ServerName everything2.com
Listen 80
ListenBacklog 511

##### MAIN VIRTUALHOST (HTTP ONLY) #####
<VirtualHost _default_:80>
	ServerName everything2.com
	ServerAdmin e2webmaster@everything2.com
	DocumentRoot /var/everything/www

	# Allow %2F (encoded slash) in URLs so nodes with '/' in their title route
	# correctly. LinkNode percent-encodes '/' to %2F; without this, Apache's
	# default (Off) 404s those URLs before the app runs. NoDecode (not On) keeps
	# %2F undecoded so _recover_route_params_from_request_uri does the single
	# decode it's designed for, and avoids the path-traversal surface On would add. (#4186)
	AllowEncodedSlashes NoDecode

	<Directory />
		Options FollowSymLinks
		AllowOverride None
	</Directory>

	# Serve favicon.ico from static directory
	Alias /favicon.ico /var/everything/www/static/favicon.ico
	<Location /favicon.ico>
		Require all granted
		SetEnv dontlog 1
	</Location>

	# Static files directory
	<Directory /var/everything/www/static/>
		Require all granted
		Options -ExecCGI
	</Directory>

	# Health check endpoint - no access logging
	<Location /health.pl>
		SetEnv dontlog 1
	</Location>

	# Apache server-status for monitoring (localhost only)
	<Location /server-status>
		SetHandler server-status
		Require local
		SetEnv dontlog 1
	</Location>

	# Explicit IP address bans
	SetEnvIf X-FORWARDED-FOR "97.66.136.146" denyip
	SetEnvIf X-FORWARDED-FOR "37.0.121.104" denyip
	SetEnvIf X-FORWARDED-FOR "183.38.89.8" denyip
	SetEnvIf X-FORWARDED-FOR "174.36.137.162" denyip
	SetEnvIf X-FORWARDED-FOR "84.238.211.161" denyip
	SetEnvIf X-FORWARDED-FOR "186.202.184.231" denyip
	SetEnvIf X-FORWARDED-FOR "168.61.72.61" denyip
	SetEnvIf X-FORWARDED-FOR "23.227.199.110" denyip
	SetEnvIf X-FORWARDED-FOR "150.129.81.30" denyip
	SetEnvIf X-FORWARDED-FOR "203.189.235.65" denyip
	SetEnvIf X-FORWARDED-FOR "103.242.1.36" denyip
	SetEnvIf X-FORWARDED-FOR "103.212.34.90" denyip
	SetEnvIf X-FORWARDED-FOR "45.125.13.32" denyip
	SetEnvIf X-FORWARDED-FOR "150.129.81.9" denyip
	SetEnvIf X-FORWARDED-FOR "31.145.16.12" denyip
	SetEnvIf X-FORWARDED-FOR "149.76.216.252" denyip
	# IP address block bans
	SetEnvIf X-FORWARDED-FOR ^221\.204 denyip
	SetEnvIf X-FORWARDED-FOR ^34\.174 denyip
	SetEnvIf X-FORWARDED-FOR ^47\.79 denyip
	SetEnvIf X-FORWARDED-FOR ^202\.46 denyip
	SetEnvIf X-FORWARDED-FOR ^220\.181 denyip
	# User agent bans
	BrowserMatchNoCase BLEXBot/1.0 denyip
	BrowserMatchNoCase DataForSeoBot/1.0 denyip
	BrowserMatchNoCase aiohttp denyip
	BrowserMatchNoCase HTTrack denyip
	BrowserMatchNoCase Baiduspider denyip


	# PSGI: Apache serves static from DocumentRoot and reverse-proxies all dynamic
	# requests to the Starman backend on :5000. app.psgi routes both pages
	# (mod_perlInit) and /api (APIRouter) from REQUEST_URI, so no title-rewrites or
	# /api ScriptAlias are needed here -- Apache runs no app code.
	ProxyPreserveHost On

	# Static assets + the local/external special routes are served by the
	# <Directory>/filesystem and the rewrites below -- NOT proxied to Starman.
	ProxyPass /css !
	ProxyPass /react !
	ProxyPass /images !
	ProxyPass /static !
	ProxyPass /js !
	ProxyPass /sound !
	ProxyPass /fonts !
	ProxyPass /favicon.ico !
	ProxyPass /robots.txt !
	ProxyPass /stylesheet !
	ProxyPass /sitemap !
	# /sitemap.xml is a sibling FILE, not under the /sitemap/ path -- Apache 2.4's
	# segment-aware ProxyPass matching does NOT treat it as covered by the
	# `/sitemap` exclusion above, so without this it falls through to Starman and
	# renders the front page instead of the S3 sitemap index. Exclude it explicitly.
	ProxyPass /sitemap.xml !
	ProxyPass /server-status !
	# Everything else -> Starman. `nocanon` passes the RAW, pre-encoded path
	# through unchanged. Apache's DEFAULT proxy canonicalization DECODES %2B->'+'
	# (and %2C->','), and then _recover_route_params_from_request_uri's tr/+/ /
	# turns that '+' into a SPACE -- so titles with a literal '+'
	# ("Writeups+plusses", "C++"), '&', etc. bounced to Findings. nocanon makes
	# Starman see exactly the pristine REQUEST_URI the mod_perl path always saw
	# (LinkNode emits literal '+' as %2B; _recover decodes it once -- a matched
	# pair). NB: this is why [P]/[NE] on a RewriteRule could not fix it -- only the
	# ProxyPass directive exposes nocanon.
	# disablereuse=On: do NOT pool/reuse backend connections. Starman is a
	# PREFORKING server with a 1s default keepalive-timeout; mod_proxy otherwise
	# keeps a backend connection in its pool, Starman closes it after 1s idle, and
	# the next reuse stalls ~1s detecting/re-establishing the dead socket -- a
	# periodic ~900ms tail-latency spike (every ~5-6 sequential requests) that
	# load testing surfaced. A fresh local-loopback connection per request is
	# ~sub-ms and is the correct model for a preforking backend anyway (it also
	# avoids keepalive connections holding Starman workers idle). Starman direct
	# (:5000) never showed the stall; only the proxied path did.
	ProxyPass / http://127.0.0.1:5000/ nocanon disablereuse=On
	ProxyPassReverse / http://127.0.0.1:5000/

	# Edge compression + the denyip ban live in <Location /> (NOT a <Directory>):
	# proxied responses have no filesystem path, so a Directory-scoped output
	# filter or access rule would never apply to the dynamic pages. Under PSGI the
	# app compresses NOTHING (compress_response_body() returns false), so Apache is
	# the sole compressor and must cover html/css/js/xml AND application/json API
	# bodies. Prefer brotli, fall back to gzip without a filter-order fight: when
	# the client accepts br, set `no-gzip` so every mod_deflate instance (the
	# global deflate.conf's and ours) stands down, leaving brotli sole; br-less
	# clients fall through to gzip. (No mod_zstd in Apache -> zstd clients get br;
	# zstd S3 assets are unaffected.)
	<Location />
		Order allow,deny
		allow from all
		deny from env=denyip
		SetEnvIfNoCase Accept-Encoding "br" no-gzip
		<IfModule mod_brotli.c>
		AddOutputFilterByType BROTLI_COMPRESS text/javascript text/html text/xml text/css application/x-javascript application/javascript application/json
		</IfModule>
		AddOutputFilterByType DEFLATE text/javascript text/html text/xml text/css application/x-javascript application/javascript application/json
	</Location>

	<Directory /var/everything/www/>
		Options +FollowSymLinks
		AllowOverride None
		Order allow,deny
		allow from all
		deny from env=denyip

		RewriteEngine On
		RewriteRule ^favicon.ico$ /static/favicon.ico [L]

		# Versioned-stylesheet 307 + sitemap external proxy. Reached because
		# /stylesheet and /sitemap are ProxyPass-excluded above.
		#
		# ProxyPreserveHost is On at the vhost level (so Starman sees the real
		# everything2.com Host). But that setting leaks into the [P] sitemap proxy
		# below, making it send `Host: everything2.com` to S3 -- which S3 reads as a
		# virtual-hosted bucket name and rejects with NoSuchBucket. Force it Off for
		# this Directory so the proxy sends `Host: sitemap.everything2.com` (the
		# bucket's vhost), exactly as the mod_perl path did (it had no
		# ProxyPreserveHost at all). ProxyPreserveHost is a per-dir directive, so this
		# override applies only here; it affects the [P] proxy -- local static serving
		# and the 307 redirect ignore it.
		ProxyPreserveHost Off
		RewriteCond %{REQUEST_FILENAME} !-f
		RewriteRule ^/?stylesheet/([^_]+)_v[0-9]+(_autofix)?\.css      /stylesheet/$1$2.css  [R=307,L]
		RewriteRule ^/?sitemap/(.+)$               http://sitemap.everything2.com/$1 [P]
		RewriteRule ^/?sitemap.xml$                http://sitemap.everything2.com/index.xml [P]
	</Directory>

	<IfDefine E2_DEVELOPMENT>
		ErrorLog "|/usr/bin/rotatelogs -f /var/log/apache2/e2.error.%Y%m%d%H.log 3600"
		CustomLog "|/usr/bin/rotatelogs -f /var/log/apache2/e2.access.%Y%m%d%H.log 3600" cloudwatch env=!dontlog
	</IfDefine>

	ServerSignature On
</VirtualHost>
